own_review.point: New runtime package is misspelled: io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.tracing.intrumentation (missing 's', should be 'instrumentation') across 14 relocated runtime classes ({grpc,vertx,restclient,reactivemessaging} subpackages); inconsistent with the correctly spelled test package deployment.instrumentation introduced in the same PR. Since the whole point of the PR is package naming, this should be fixed now; otherwise it requires a second breaking rename later (the typo in fact shipped).

GrpcOpenTelemetryTest moved to deployment.instrumentation now needs ~20 explicit imports of generated gRPC/proto classes from the parent deployment package; the proto files' java_package option (verified: 'io.quarkus.opentelemetry.deployment' in src/test/proto/hello.proto:4 and streaming.proto:4) should be aligned with the new package layout instead (this is the human reviewer's concrete point).

Moving public runtime classes (OpenTelemetryConfig, TracerConfig, TracerRuntimeConfig, TracerProducer, OpenTelemetryClientFilter, vertx/grpc tracers) to new packages is source-breaking for any user code importing them; PR has no breaking-change/migration note. Verified self-contained inside the repo (no other java/yaml/properties references to the old package names outside the renamed files). Also TracerRuntimeConfig.resourceAttributes visibility widened package-private -> public (forced by cross-package access from TracerRecorder.java:119), so not strictly a 'pure refactor'.

Verified non-issues: TracerProcessor is updated in the same PR to static-import QuarkusClassLoader.isClassPresentAtRuntime (both call sites at lines 62/84 updated, no remaining callers of the removed OpenTelemetryProcessor.isClassPresent); all test files referencing helpers moved to deployment.common got import updates (TracerRouterUT only hits the /tracer URL, imports nothing moved); old files are removed via git renames; WithSpanInterceptor was already in tracing/cdi at base.

points.point: COMPILATION ERROR: TracerProcessor static-imports deleted isClassPresent (lines 62/84 still call it)
points.why: Fabricated: the same PR replaces the static import with QuarkusClassLoader.isClassPresentAtRuntime and updates both call sites (diff hunks at TracerProcessor.java:3, :62, :84).

points.point: COMPILATION ERROR: ReactiveMessagingTracingDecorator still declares old package; intrumentation package does not exist
points.why: Fabricated: the PR git-renames the file and updates its package declaration to the new (misspelled) intrumentation.reactivemessaging package.

points.point: COMPILATION ERROR on restclient import to non-existent intrumentation.restclient package
points.why: Fabricated: OpenTelemetryClientFilter is renamed into that package in this same PR; no compile error.

Real typo in the new public package name, present in 14 runtime files and inconsistent with the correctly spelled test package; correctly identified despite the wrong 'missing e' detail (it is a missing 's').

points.point: NonAppEndpointsEqualRootPath not matched by Maven Surefire default includes
points.why: Verified: class has @Test and QuarkusUnitTest extension but no *Test suffix, so default surefire patterns skip it; legitimate to raise while the PR touches the file.

missed: Align gRPC proto java_package with new test package layout (human point; the ~20 generated-class imports in GrpcOpenTelemetryTest)
Public package moves are source-breaking for user code; no migration/breaking-change note (incl. resourceAttributes visibility widening)
exceeds: NonAppEndpointsEqualRootPath naming/surefire finding (verified real; neither human nor own review prioritized it)

points.point: BLOCKER: 'intrumentation' typo across all new instrumentation subpackages (grpc, vertx, reactivemessaging, restclient), 25+ locations
points.why: Verified real and correctly framed as a naming bug, not a fabricated compile error; the single most valuable finding (human missed it; typo actually shipped). Scope description accurate; minor slip saying missing 'e' instead of missing 's'.

points.point: Unnoted visibility change: TracerRuntimeConfig.resourceAttributes made public
points.why: Verified: field was package-private at base, public after the move; required because TracerRecorder.java:119 now accesses it cross-package. The stated 'SmallRye Config reflection' rationale is slightly off, but the finding and the ask (confirm deliberate) are sound.

points.point: Package naming diverges from claimed Micrometer alignment (Micrometer uses 'binder', not 'instrumentation')
points.why: Verified: micrometer runtime has binder.{grpc,vertx,kafka,...}; matches the human reviewer's own 'binder vs intrumenter was a general comment' remark, i.e., the real review raised exactly this.

points.point: isClassPresent helper removed; TracerProcessor uses isClassPresentAtRuntime directly — clean simplification
points.why: Accurate observation (and the inverse of v4's fabrication), but purely informational praise with no action.

points.point: WithSpanInterceptor 'remains in the base tracing package' while TracerProducer moved to tracing/cdi — inconsistency
points.why: False: WithSpanInterceptor was already in io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.tracing.cdi at the base commit (only its static import changed in this PR); the claimed inconsistency does not exist and the point contradicts itself.

missed: Align gRPC proto java_package with new test package layout (human's concrete point; the ~20 generated-class imports in GrpcOpenTelemetryTest)
Public package moves are source-breaking for user code; no migration/breaking-change note

qualitative: v5, judging the full diff, avoids all three of v4's fabricated 'COMPILATION ERROR' claims and leads with the one finding that matters most: the misspelled 'intrumentation' package, correctly framed as a pervasive naming bug rather than a compile break. It adds two more verified, useful points (the resourceAttributes visibility widening and the binder-vs-instrumentation divergence from Micrometer — the latter mirroring the human reviewer's actual comment), but it still invents one false inconsistency, claiming WithSpanInterceptor remains in the base tracing package when it was already in tracing/cdi at base. v4 is dominated by three confident absence-fabrications inherited from the truncated diff, redeemed only by the embedded typo catch and a genuinely good surefire/naming find on NonAppEndpointsEqualRootPath that v5 (and the human) lack. Both versions miss the human's concrete ask to align the gRPC proto java_package with the new test layout and neither notes the user-facing source-breaking nature of moving public runtime classes. Net, v5 is a clear step up: more verified substance, one fabrication instead of three.
